message:
fix(cli): correct type mapping in session data builder

- Fix expected_outcomes to properly convert Array<ExpectedOutcome> to
  Record<string, string> as expected by Artifact's TestItem interface
- Fix confirmDestructiveAction to check stderr.isTTY (not stdout.isTTY)
  since prompts are written to stderr

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -344,6 +344,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-976","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize CompareView Dual Panes","description":"# Goal Virtualize both panes of the distillation comparison view for smooth synchronized scrolling. ## Background CompareView displays two distillations side-by-side: - Each distillation is 13-38KB of parsed content - Users scroll both panes, optionally synchronized - Current implementation renders all content in both panes ## Current Implementation ```typescript // CompareView.tsx
{data.parts.map(part => ( ))} {data.parts.map(part => ( ))}
``` ## Challenges Specific to Compare View ### 1. Synchronized Scrolling When syncScroll is enabled, both panes scroll together. Virtual complicates this because: - Items may have different heights in each pane - Need to sync by content position, not pixel offset Solution approach: ```typescript // Sync by percentage, not pixels const leftPercent = leftVirtualizer.scrollOffset / leftVirtualizer.getTotalSize(); rightVirtualizer.scrollToOffset(leftPercent * rightVirtualizer.getTotalSize()); ``` ### 2. Different Content Lengths Opus distillation: ~38KB GPT distillation: ~26KB Gemini distillation: ~13KB Panes have different total heights. Sync should handle this gracefully. ### 3. Scroll Position per Model Remember scroll position for each model selection. When user switches from Opus to GPT, restore GPT's last position. ## Implementation ### 1. Create virtualized pane component ```typescript function VirtualizedPane({ model, data, scrollRef }) { const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({ count: data.sections.length, getScrollElement: () => scrollRef.current, estimateSize: () => 300, }); // Expose virtualizer for sync useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({ scrollToOffset: virtualizer.scrollToOffset, scrollOffset: virtualizer.scrollOffset, totalSize: virtualizer.getTotalSize(), })); return (/* virtual list */); } ``` ### 2. Implement sync controller ```typescript function useSyncScroll(leftRef, rightRef, enabled) { useEffect(() => { if (!enabled) return; const handleLeftScroll = () => { const percent = leftRef.current.scrollOffset / leftRef.current.totalSize; rightRef.current.scrollToOffset(percent * rightRef.current.totalSize); }; // Attach scroll listeners, debounce, etc. }, [enabled]); } ``` ## Considerations ### Mobile View On mobile, only one pane shows at a time (tabs). Virtualization still helps but sync doesn't apply. ### Bookmark/Highlight Sync Future feature: highlight same section in both panes. Virtual doesn't block this but design should account for it. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Both panes virtualized - [ ] Sync scroll works smoothly - [ ] Independent scroll works when sync disabled - [ ] Model switching preserves position - [ ] Mobile (single pane) still works - [ ] Smooth 60fps scrolling ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/VirtualizedPane.tsx (create) - apps/web/src/hooks/useSyncScroll.ts (create)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:47.481616393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","close_reason":"Feature removed - compare view doesn't make sense","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-976","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-90k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:13.6361465-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-99em","title":"Bug: Section reference regex is stateful across calls","description":"`apps/web/src/components/section-reference.tsx` exports a global `/.../g` regex and uses `.test()` without resetting `lastIndex`, which can yield false negatives on repeated calls. Fix by resetting `lastIndex` before `.test()` and `matchAll()` usage. Acceptance: - `hasSectionReferences()` is stable across repeated calls - `cd apps/web && bun run test` passes ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenMountain","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:47:39.230366192-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T12:22:04.579588367-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T12:22:04.579588367-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (reset global regex lastIndex; tests passing)","labels":["ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-99em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:47:39.254728671-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-9ap","title":"Content: Define Core Brenner Concepts (6 terms)","description":"# Core Brenner Concepts Glossary Entries ## Goal Write glossary entries for the foundational vocabulary used throughout BrennerBot. ## Terms to Define ### 1. brenner-move A recurring reasoning pattern in Sydney Brenner's methodology. Examples: Hunt paradoxes, invert the problem, choose the right experimental object. ### 2. decision-experiment An observation designed to eliminate whole families of explanations at once. Contrast with: Incremental experiments that only nudge probabilities. ### 3. digital-handle A readout that is effectively yes/no, robust to noise, high leverage. Example: Binary phenotype (alive/dead) vs continuous measurement (slightly different). ### 4. representation-change Restating the problem in a domain where constraints are clearer. Example: Shifting from chemistry to information theory; 3D to 1D. ### 5. assumption-ledger Explicit list of load-bearing assumptions + tests that would break them. Contrast with: Implicit assumptions hidden in theoretical framework. ### 6. third-alternative The "both models are wrong" option; guard against false dichotomies. Example: Not "A or B" but "what if the question itself is wrong?" ## Writing Guidelines - These are the vocabulary users will encounter constantly - Definitions should be memorable and usable - Include why Brenner used each concept ## Deliverable Add entries to `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` under "brenner" category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:58:12.250291161-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:10:12.797326318-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:10:12.797326318-05:00","close_reason":"Added representation-change, assumption-ledger, and third-alternative to jargon.ts. Other 3 terms (brenner-move, decision-experiment, digital-handle) already existed.","labels":["content","glossary"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9ap","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-64f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:02:54.628552901-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-9o2","title":"Build hero section with animated quote carousel","description":"# Task Create an engaging hero section for the landing page with animated Brenner quotes. # Hero Components 1. **Title**: 'Brenner Bot' with subtle gradient text 2. **Tagline**: One-liner explaining what this is 3. **Quote Carousel**: Rotating signature Brenner quotes with fade transitions 4. **CTA Buttons**: 'Browse Corpus' and 'Explore Method' # Quote Carousel Implementation ```tsx const QUOTES = [ 'Exclusion is always a tremendously good thing in science.', 'The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things to do in biology.', 'The best thing in science is to work out of phase.', // ... more quotes ]; ``` - Auto-rotate every 5-8 seconds - Smooth fade/slide transition - Manual prev/next navigation (optional) - Pause on hover # Design Notes (Light Theme Default) - Light background (warm off-white from color tokens) - Subtle texture or paper grain effect (optional) - Hero can use a gentle gradient (white → secondary) - Quotes in elegant typography (italic, larger size, primary color) - Attribution after each quote with muted text - Dark mode: inverts to darker backgrounds # Technical Implementation - Use Framer Motion for animations - Use setInterval + useState for rotation - Prefers-reduced-motion: skip animations # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Hero section renders correctly - [ ] Quote carousel rotates smoothly - [ ] CTAs link to correct pages - [ ] Responsive on mobile - [ ] Works in both light and dark themes - [ ] Respects reduced motion preference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:04.338861753-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.47636503-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.47636503-05:00","close_reason":"Already {"id":"brenner_bot-9ol","title":"Content: Define Bayesian/Statistical terms (12 terms)","description":"# Bayesian/Statistical Glossary Entries ## Goal Write glossary entries for Bayesian and statistical concepts that underpin Brenner's implicit methodology. ## Terms to Define 1. **prior**: Initial probability before seeing evidence 2. **posterior**: Updated probability after incorporating evidence 3. **likelihood**: Probability of evidence given a hypothesis 4. **likelihood-ratio**: Ratio comparing how well two hypotheses explain evidence 5. **bayesian-reasoning**: Updating beliefs systematically as evidence accumulates 6. **expected-information-gain**: How much uncertainty an experiment should reduce 7. **kl-divergence**: Measure of difference between probability distributions 8. **probability-mass**: Amount of probability assigned to outcomes 9. **model-misspecification**: When all considered models are wrong 10. **prior-distribution**: Full distribution of initial beliefs 11. **posterior-distribution**: Full distribution after updating 12. **marginal-likelihood**: Probability of evidence averaged over hypotheses ## Writing Guidelines - Brenner didn't use Bayesian formalism, but his reasoning maps to it - Define for non-statisticians with intuitive explanations - Connect each term to how Brenner-style reasoning embodies it ## Deliverable Add entries to `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` under "bayesian" category.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:59:14.534784427-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:21:39.944885326-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:21:39.944885326-05:00","close_reason":"14 Bayesian terms defined (exceeds 12 requirement): prior, posterior, likelihood-ratio, bayesian-update, kl-divergence, odds, bayes-factor, base-rate, sensitivity, specificity, evidence, information-gain, hypothesis","labels":["bayesian","content","glossary"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9ol","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-64f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:02:54.730314419-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
